title: chrysothamnus parryi var. attenuatus
intro: Chrysothamnus parryi var. attenuatus, also known as narrow-leaved chamisa, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. Narrow-leaved chamisa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use.
